free_text: Reactor production of promethium-147
Irradiations were made in the core of
ORNL High Flux Isotope Reactor (HFIR)
Target in form of neodymium oxyde  Nd2O3 in synthetic
quarz ampoules with 6 mm  outer diameter, 4 mm inner
diameter and ID ~40 mm long. Seven targets with two
different enrichments were used. Targets with lower
enrichment (86.8% for 146Nd) were used mainly for
development of chemical separation procedure
Gamma rays were detected using two HPGe
detectors with active volume 10 and 100 cm3 with
resolution 1 keV at 123 keV 1.8 and 1.9 KeV at 1332
keV, respectively.
Cross sections were determined by
analysing product yields and their decay variation.
Irradiations were performed in center of reactor
core with thermal neutron flux up to 2.1E+15 n/cm2/s,
with thermal to epithermal flux ratio of 30+-1.5 and
fast (En > 1 MeV) to thermal ratio approx. 0.25.
Origin of uncertainties not described
Compiled by S.H.
